name: Ultralight scalar dark matter
summary: Dark matter is a coherent oscillation of an ultralight scalar field (m ~ 10^-22 - 10^-18 eV) that behaves classically on galactic scales. The de Broglie wavelength at galactic velocities is kpc-scale for m ~ 10^-22 eV, smearing out small-scale structure and addressing claimed discrepancies (cusp/core, missing satellites). Includes axion-like particles in the sub-eV range.

addresses_puzzles.note: Lyman-alpha forest constraints push m > ~2e-21 eV for the fuzzy regime; halo-shape and pulsar-timing constraints further squeeze. Slightly heavier (10^-15 - sub-eV) ALPs remain viable across a wide range of f_a.

touches_benchmarks.note: Distinct from the WDM thermal-mass bound stored as the benchmark current_bound (5.3 keV). For ultralight bosonic DM (fuzzy regime), Iršič et al. 2017 (arXiv:1703.04683) give the conservative bound m_ULDM > ~2e-21 eV from XQ-100 + HIRES/MIKE quasar spectra. The two limits constrain different DM particle classes and are not directly interchangeable.
ALP DM with photon coupling targeted by ADMX-type haloscopes.
Coupling to dilaton-like scalar predicts oscillating alpha at frequency m_phi; atomic-clock comparison searches set bounds.

introduces.note: Mass, decay constant, initial misalignment angle.

history: Hu-Barkana-Gruzinov (2000) introduced fuzzy DM; renewed interest 2010s with high-resolution simulations and ALP cosmology. Tight Lyman-alpha bounds (Iršič et al. 2017) constrain the lightest fuzzy regime.

references.citation: Hu, Barkana, Gruzinov, 'Cold and Fuzzy Dark Matter' (also: 'Fuzzy Cold Dark Matter: The Wave Properties of Ultralight Particles'), PRL 85, 1158 (2000)
Iršič et al., 'First constraints on fuzzy dark matter from Lyman-alpha forest data and hydrodynamical simulations', PRL 119, 031302 (2017)
